jokes.text: Stanley wearing a costume so he can sleep at his desk
jokes.setup: Stanley claims to hate Halloween but is wearing a costume
jokes.punchline: The practical reason - it allows him to sleep at work undetected
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - reveals Stanley's lazy nature through clever exploitation of Halloween. Economical setup/payoff with excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Carrie Bradshaw character asking for help walking to the fax machine
jokes.setup: Someone dressed as glamorous Carrie Bradshaw
jokes.punchline: Needs assistance for the mundane office task of using the fax machine
jokes.explanation: Mild costume humor contrasting glamorous character with mundane office reality.

jokes.text: Gordon Gekko 'From the insurance commercials'
jokes.setup: Character claims to be Gordon Gekko from Wall Street
jokes.punchline: Other character thinks it's from insurance commercials, missing the cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing cultural awareness gap, economical delivery.

jokes.text: Let's put a smile on that face
jokes.setup: Someone in Joker costume
jokes.punchline: Delivers the iconic Joker line
jokes.explanation: Low-effort pop culture reference, predictable costume/quote pairing.

jokes.text: Damn it, Creed. I've been up since 4:00
jokes.setup: Creed has apparently done something annoying with his costume
jokes.punchline: Someone's frustrated reaction about being up early, implying Creed's costume is elaborate or disturbing
jokes.explanation: Typical Creed causing chaos, good character integration suggesting his costume is characteristically weird.

jokes.text: Who are you supposed to be? Dave. Cool.
jokes.setup: Andy asks about someone's costume
jokes.punchline: They're dressed as 'Dave' - just a regular guy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-costume joke with perfect economy. Subverts expectation of elaborate Halloween costume with mundane normalcy.

jokes.text: A cat? We were looking for kitten
jokes.setup: Pam apparently dressed as a cat
jokes.punchline: Jim's correction suggesting they coordinated as 'kitten' rather than 'cat'
jokes.explanation: Cute couples costume wordplay, shows their relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: My costume's getting a lot of attention. So apparently no one dresses up for Halloween here
jokes.setup: Jim claims his costume is getting attention
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's because he's the only one in costume
jokes.explanation: Good ironic revelation with classic Jim self-deprecating awareness. Well-structured misdirect.

jokes.text: I can't even take off my hat because then I'm Hitler
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his costume troubles with greasepaint mustache
jokes.punchline: Removing his hat would reveal an accidental Hitler look
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy with perfect escalation. The casual delivery of such an uncomfortable realization is pitch-perfect Office humor.

jokes.text: Want to see a magic trick? I'm going to make a pencil... disappear
jokes.setup: Someone in Joker costume sets up magic trick
jokes.punchline: References the famous Dark Knight pencil trick scene
jokes.explanation: Another predictable pop culture reference. Low originality, relies entirely on recognition.

jokes.text: Look at all this stuff. It's only seven hours
jokes.setup: Michael has packed elaborate road trip entertainment
jokes.punchline: Holly points out it's excessive for a seven-hour drive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's over-preparation and Holly's practical nature.

jokes.text: Those Duke boys are at it again. Hey, do not touch my radio
jokes.setup: Darryl plays along with CB radio talk
jokes.punchline: Michael's protective reaction about his radio being touched
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's possessiveness and control issues in a lighthearted context.

jokes.text: I said 'Jinx' and then we never talked about it again
jokes.setup: Holly and Michael argued about who should quit their job
jokes.punchline: The serious relationship discussion ended with 'Jinx' and was never revisited
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd relationship dynamic. The idea that a life-changing conversation ends with a childhood game is perfectly ridiculous and character-specific.

jokes.text: Or maybe to beat me up. I can never tell with those two
jokes.setup: Jim's brothers are taking them to celebrate the engagement
jokes.punchline: Jim genuinely can't distinguish their celebratory intentions from violent ones
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic humor, establishes the brothers' intimidating nature with efficient delivery.

jokes.text: Take that sweatshirt off
jokes.setup: Dwight shows up wearing Cornell colors casually
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate territorial reaction
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Cornell rivalry storyline well, very in-character for Andy's school pride obsession.

jokes.text: Those colors are sacred. Not that I care, but if you're not a Cornell man, you probably shouldn't wear them
jokes.setup: Andy claims the colors are sacred
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by saying he doesn't care
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy contradiction - clearly does care deeply while claiming not to. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: If someone who barely outsells Phyllis can get in, then I should be fine. I'm sitting right here, Dwight
jokes.setup: Dwight insults Andy by comparing his sales to Phyllis
jokes.punchline: Phyllis points out she's present for the insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight tactlessness with good Phyllis reaction. Shows multiple character dynamics efficiently.

jokes.text: I meant that as a compliment to you, Phyllis, as well as a slight to Andy
jokes.setup: Dwight tries to recover from insulting Phyllis
jokes.punchline: His 'explanation' makes it worse by being completely honest about his intentions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - his attempt at diplomacy reveals his calculating nature. Excellent character integration with authentic voice.

jokes.text: Life is a highway I wanna ride it all night long
jokes.setup: Michael and Holly singing together in the car
jokes.punchline: The earnest duet of a cheesy road trip song
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic road trip moment, somewhat cliché but fits their relationship.

jokes.text: Stop saying 'we.' You did not go to Cornell, okay?
jokes.setup: Dwight keeps using 'we' when discussing Cornell
jokes.punchline: Andy's frustrated correction about Dwight's lack of affiliation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Cornell rivalry, shows Andy's territorial nature about his school identity.

jokes.text: Without its agricultural program, we probably wouldn't have cabbage, at least not modern cabbage
jokes.setup: Dwight defending Cornell's excellence
jokes.punchline: His specific example is bizarrely focused on cabbage development
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Dwight absurdity - his agricultural obsession leads to a completely ridiculous but confidently delivered claim about cabbage history. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: My blood runs Big Red
jokes.setup: Andy expressing his Cornell loyalty
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top passionate declaration
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy over-enthusiasm, shows his school spirit obsession but not particularly original.

jokes.text: That Pam Beesly, she's the coolest sister-in-law on the planet. She's the best, the absolute best
jokes.setup: Pam imagining what Jim's brothers think of her prank idea
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top fantasy of their approval
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Pam's insecurity about fitting in with Jim's family, mild cringe humor.

jokes.text: Remember that thing we did when Jim was in high school with his girlfriend?
jokes.setup: Pam suggests a prank about losing her engagement ring
jokes.punchline: The brothers immediately pivot to a meaner prank from Jim's past
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the brothers being meaner than expected, subverts Pam's harmless prank with something darker.

jokes.text: Like, she basically has a hobby for a job
jokes.setup: The brothers targeting Pam's art career
jokes.punchline: The cutting dismissal of art as just a hobby
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment, the casual cruelty feels realistic for obnoxious brothers.

jokes.text: They came up with that idea really fast
jokes.setup: The brothers immediately switching to a meaner prank
jokes.punchline: Pam's uncomfortable realization that they're practiced at being cruel
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction showing Pam's dawning awareness of their true nature. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Emphasis on the bed and the breakfast
jokes.setup: Michael wants to find a bed and breakfast for their meetings
jokes.punchline: His not-so-subtle sexual emphasis
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael sexual humor, predictable but in character.

jokes.text: I just imagined a hotel right here, pool over here, little breakfast place with really good bacon
jokes.setup: They're stopped in the middle of nowhere
jokes.punchline: Michael's detailed fantasy of amenities that obviously aren't there
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's ability to fantasy-project onto reality, visual contrast with desolate location.

jokes.text: Did Darryl touch you?
jokes.setup: Holly seems upset about something
jokes.punchline: Michael's bizarre leap to Darryl being inappropriately physical
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Michael logic - his mind jumps to the most random explanation. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: In the last 10 years I've dated almost four women, and you are so far above them, it is stupid
jokes.setup: Michael trying to express how special Holly is
jokes.punchline: The accidentally pathetic revelation of his dating history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - trying to be romantic but accidentally revealing how sad his dating life is. 'Almost four' is brilliantly specific.

jokes.text: I'm going to make this way harder than it needs to be
jokes.setup: Holly says not to make the breakup harder than it needs to be
jokes.punchline: Michael's defiant declaration to do exactly that
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - Michael's inability to handle things maturely expressed with perfect honesty.

jokes.text: It's pronounced 'Colonel.' It's the highest rank in the military. It's pronounced 'Cornell.' It's the highest rank in the Ivy League
jokes.setup: Dwight deliberately mispronounces Cornell as Colonel
jokes.punchline: Andy's correction with parallel structure about Ivy League ranking
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay battle with perfect parallel structure. Both characters get strong moments showing their competitive nature.

jokes.text: That's Big Red Bear. That's a bobble Big Red Bear. God!
jokes.setup: Andy getting increasingly agitated about Cornell
jokes.punchline: His overwrought reaction to Dwight's Cornell merchandise
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Andy's frustration, shows his school obsession reaching breaking point.

jokes.text: My wish has come true, incidentally, because you've met me and you are happy
jokes.setup: Michael wishes Holly would meet a great guy and be happy
jokes.punchline: His immediate pivot to make it about himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott logic - starts noble and immediately becomes self-serving. Excellent structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Maybe Pam should pay the check by drawing a picture on this napkin
jokes.setup: Brothers attacking Pam's art career prospects
jokes.punchline: Suggesting her art is worthless enough to be payment
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor, the casual cruelty feels authentic for bullying brothers.

jokes.text: We're like peanut butter and jelly. Please don't do this! Please don't do this!
jokes.setup: Michael making romantic analogies
jokes.punchline: Immediate desperate begging when Holly wants to leave
jokes.explanation: Good emotional whiplash showing Michael's desperation, effective cringe moment.

jokes.text: I'll go back to Jan, and I hate Jan
jokes.setup: Michael's desperate voicemail about needing Holly
jokes.punchline: His threat to return to his terrible ex-relationship
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jan relationship, shows Michael's self-destructive patterns and desperate logic.

jokes.text: I called admissions and it looks like I will be conducting your university interview
jokes.setup: Dwight asking about Cornell a cappella groups
jokes.punchline: Andy's power move of becoming his interviewer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their rivalry, shows Andy using his Cornell connection strategically.

jokes.text: That's a conflict of interest. Yeah. Big one
jokes.setup: Dwight points out Andy's conflict of interest
jokes.punchline: Andy's casual acknowledgment that he doesn't care
jokes.explanation: Good understated humor showing Andy's pettiness, economical delivery.

jokes.text: When the hour glass strikes 3:00, then in the room whence employees confer
jokes.setup: Andy scheduling the interview
jokes.punchline: His pompous, pseudo-medieval language for '3 o'clock in the conference room'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy pretentiousness, the unnecessarily flowery language is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: If I had to put Dwight's chances into a percentage, I would say he has none percent chance
jokes.setup: Andy assessing Dwight's admission chances
jokes.punchline: The contradictory phrasing of 'none percent chance'
jokes.explanation: Good awkward phrasing that sounds professional but is actually nonsensical.

jokes.text: Dane Cook, Jack Bauer and Eli Whitney
jokes.setup: Andy asks about role models
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre combination of a comedian, fictional character, and historical inventor
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random Dwight answer - the completely unrelated trio shows his strange thought process. Perfect absurdist humor.

jokes.text: She only knows how to play When the Saints Go Marching In
jokes.setup: Tom showing pride in his trumpeter daughter
jokes.punchline: The limitation that she only knows one song
jokes.explanation: Mild parental humor about limited musical abilities, relatable but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Miles Davis. One. Chet something. Half
jokes.setup: Tom challenges naming famous trumpeters besides Louis Armstrong
jokes.punchline: Jim's scoring system for partially remembered names
jokes.explanation: Clever response showing Jim's quick thinking, good structure with the scoring system.

jokes.text: We pranked you. It was Pam's idea. Pam was the mastermind
jokes.setup: The entire lunch conversation was attacks on Pam
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was supposedly all a prank orchestrated by Pam
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirect but feels somewhat forced, the brothers' cruelty seemed too genuine.

jokes.text: Dale Raymond Corson. I'm sorry, that's incorrect. Cornell's seventh president was, in fact, James A. Perkins
jokes.setup: Andy asks about Cornell's eighth president
jokes.punchline: Dwight gets it wrong but Andy's correction reveals his own mistake
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' obsessive knowledge while revealing Andy's mistake in his own question.

jokes.text: Applicant has a head shaped like a trapezoid
jokes.setup: Andy and Dwight trading insults in their fake evaluations
jokes.punchline: Andy's petty personal attack about Dwight's head shape
jokes.explanation: Good petty insult that fits the escalating rivalry, specific geometric reference is funny.

jokes.text: Ever heard of it?
jokes.setup: Dwight threatens to go to Dartmouth instead
jokes.punchline: His condescending question about whether Andy knows Dartmouth
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight condescension, economical delivery of his superiority complex.

jokes.text: I had this whole thing where I go back to class, wrongly accuse another girl. Look, I even used makeup to put a ring around my finger
jokes.setup: Pam explaining her elaborate prank plan
jokes.punchline: The detailed preparation including a fake tan line
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Pam's elaborate prank planning and attention to detail.

jokes.text: Text message from my brother. 'Pam cool. Welcome to the family.'
jokes.setup: Jim checking his phone after the lunch
jokes.punchline: The simple, positive message contrasting with the brothers' behavior
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution showing the brothers' approval, economical payoff to the lunch storyline.

jokes.text: How about at Thanksgiving we 'prank' Tom about being bald?
jokes.setup: Jim suggesting a future prank
jokes.punchline: The air quotes around 'prank' suggesting it would just be mean
jokes.explanation: Good Jim sarcasm showing his understanding of his brothers' cruel 'pranks.'

jokes.text: Sometimes when I'm down like this, it helps to sing the blues
jokes.setup: Michael is depressed about Holly leaving
jokes.punchline: Darryl's earnest attempt to help through blues music
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Darryl's genuine attempt to comfort Michael.

jokes.text: Bernard Farms. Best beets in the state
jokes.setup: Someone asks where Dwight got raw beets he's eating
jokes.punchline: His proud promotion of his beet farm
jokes.explanation: Standard Dwight beet farm reference, good character consistency but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You're supposed to cook these, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Dwight is eating raw beets
jokes.punchline: Someone's reasonable question about cooking them first
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about Dwight's weird eating habits.
